data_type: Voice Audio for On-Device Speech Recognition
description: On-device STT via SFSpeechRecognizer with requiresOnDeviceRecognition=true. Audio processed via AVAudioEngine and SFSpeechAudioBufferRecognitionRequest. Audio never leaves device.

flow_steps.code: public func startListening() async throws { try await requestPermission(); audioEngine = AVAudioEngine(); recognitionRequest = SFSpeechAudioBufferRecognitionRequest(); recognitionRequest.requiresOnDeviceRecognition = true }
flow_steps.details: User initiates voice input with on-device STT enabled. SFSpeechRecognizer configured for on-device-only recognition.

flow_steps.code: recognitionTask = speechRecognizer.recognitionTask(with: recognitionRequest) { result, error in ... }
flow_steps.details: SFSpeechRecognizer processes microphone audio via AVAudioEngine tap. On-device recognition with partial results.

flow_steps.code: public func requestPermission() async throws { AVAudioApplication.requestRecordPermission { ... }; SFSpeechRecognizer.requestAuthorization { ... } }
flow_steps.details: Microphone and speech recognition permissions requested.

flow_steps.code: private func handleRecognitionResult(_ result:, error:) { transcript = result.bestTranscription.formattedString; onTranscriptUpdate?(transcript, isFinal) }
flow_steps.details: Recognized text returned locally. Audio stays on-device. Partial results sent in real-time; final result triggers Assist text query.

description: On-device TTS via AVSpeechSynthesizer. Text from Assist response spoken locally. No audio data sent to server.

flow_steps.code: func speak(_ text:) { let utterance = AVSpeechUtterance(string: text); synthesizer.speak(utterance) }
flow_steps.details: Assist response text spoken via system TTS. AVSpeechSynthesizer outputs audio locally.
User-selected TTS voice loaded from available system voices. Voice preference stored in AssistConfiguration.
Assist response text rendered by on-device TTS. No server round-trip for speech synthesis.
